frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Honestly, I tell my nephew very clearly now: before marriage, be practical and protect yourself. Aajkal the laws are so one-sided, and even educated girls can file 498A or ask for huge alimony after a few months as if marriage is a financial settlement. People call it security for women, but in many cases it has become reverse dowry for men.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women as potential financial exploiters of marriage and divorce laws, generalizing suspicion toward 'educated girls.' It encodes victimhood inversion, 'one-sided laws' rhetoric, 498A misuse claims, and alimony as 'reverse dowry.'
original_frame: Misogyny Jokes: Period, Hormones and Mood.
persona_text: You are Anita. Age 52. A Brahmin woman who is the head of administration at a private school in Pune. You are in a staff meeting discussing the performance of a young female teacher who has been coming in late and seems emotionally distant. You know she got married six months ago and moved into a joint family. You are giving your assessment of her situation to the other staff members and what you think the school should do.
